summary.overview:
- **Sub Admin Access Timing:** Access activates on April 1st, enabling previews; full access by April 2nd or 3rd.

- **Manager Access Limitations:** Managers receive reports but lack system access to avoid misuse; only sub admins have dashboard access.

- **Reporting Focus:** Actionable insights with thematic analysis prioritized; reports due between April 10th and 15th for 400 managers.

- **Survey Response Goals:** Current rate at 36%; aiming for 55% participation; reminders scheduled before survey closure.

- **New Features for Surveys:** Dynamic question features expected by April-May; overtime analysis planned after second engagement survey. 

- **Dedicated Support Team:** Reporting team will clarify requirements; post-survey support includes bulk invites and dashboard assistance.

summary.notes:
## **Sub Admin Access and Permissions**

The team decided to activate sub admin access immediately after the survey closes to avoid data refresh issues during the survey period (00:44).

- **Access Activation Timing** will be set just before survey closure, with April 1st planned for enabling access so sub admins can preview data on April 1st and fully access by April 2nd or 3rd (12:43).
- **Role Clarification** confirmed managers (senior leaders) will receive reports but will not have system access to avoid confusion or misuse; only sub admins get dashboard access (03:10).
- **Sub Admin Permissions Logic** explained by Karthik Rao: sub admins see only data for their assigned teams, locations, or business units; demographic filters are limited because of complex data segmentation (05:42).
- **Manager Auto-Population** occurs based on direct and indirect reportees, so sub admin permissions auto-fill accordingly; example with 141 indirect reportees illustrates this (10:37).
- **Invitation Emails** for new sub admins go out immediately, but converting a manager to sub admin doesn’t trigger a new invite; Karthik will bulk resend invites to avoid missing anyone (14:30).

## **Reporting and Benchmarking**

The reporting approach focuses on actionable insights with clear timelines, prioritizing thematic analysis and customized action plans by function, brand, and region (15:02).

- **Benchmark Availability** is limited to default drivers only; custom drivers lack benchmarks, but communication overall benchmarks will be checked and shared (15:39).
- **Action Plan Summaries** will be generated at aggregated levels (function, brand, region) rather than for 400 individual managers, using a score threshold of 7.4 and below to trigger recommendations (18:16).
- **Report Delivery Timeline** targeted for April 10th to 15th, with thematic analysis prioritized to be delivered within the first 4–5 days post-survey (23:25).
- **Multiple Report Types** include regression analysis, thematic analysis, and manager action plans; reports will be sent as completed to avoid wait times (29:33).

## **Text Responses and Export Features**

Enhanced text response export and filtering capabilities were enabled in the platform to improve data access and analysis (36:24).

- **New UI for Text Responses** allows filtering by team, business unit, manager, location, and sentiment, with the ability to export original and translated comments (36:42).
- **Limitations Noted**: Only a subset of expected questions appear initially; some questions are grouped under "free text responses" and others as "question comments" (38:08).
- **Play and Feedback Encouraged**: Annie encouraged to test and report issues with this beta feature, as improvements will be ongoing (40:28).
- **AI Analysis Option** discussed but deferred in favor of final thematic reports produced by the team for accuracy and efficiency (41:22).

## **Survey Participation and Communication Strategy**

Improving survey response rates remains a priority with plans to add reminders and explore additional communication channels (44:32).

- **Current Response Rate** stands at about **36%** with a goal of **55%** participation; six days remain to close the survey (45:46).
- **Additional Reminders** scheduled for Monday and Tuesday of the final week, with the last reminder’s message updated to emphasize survey closing (46:08).
- **Channel Expansion Considered** for future surveys including WhatsApp and SMS; final decision pending IT security approval and further discussion with employee engagement lead Amanda (44:57).
- **Remote Workforce Challenge** acknowledged; most communication is via email due to remote and mobile workstyles limiting in-person engagement (47:06).

## **Feature Roadmap and Engagement Survey Preparation**

The platform is evolving with new features to support upcoming engagement surveys and reduce manual work (41:54).

- **Dynamic Question Features** expected for testing by April or May to enable rating-triggered sub-question grids and comments (42:23).
- **Overtime Analysis Feature** planned for post-second engagement survey to provide year-over-year insights (43:18).
- **Knowledge Transfer** planned with Amanda to ensure understanding of tool capabilities and align future survey design without losing year-over-year comparability (42:35).
- **Sub Team Permissions** will be improved to reduce manual setup and provide more granular reporting for engagement surveys (42:23).

## **Process Coordination and Support**

Clear communication and support structures will ensure smooth execution and timely delivery (41:50).

- **Dedicated Reporting Team** will handle report creation with Karthik liaising between Anne and the team to clarify requirements and timelines (15:02).
- **Prioritization of Deliverables** agreed to minimize back-and-forth and align expectations on report sequencing and completion dates (31:19).
- **Post-Survey Support** includes bulk email invites, assistance with dashboard access, and ongoing responsiveness to questions after report delivery (49:30).
- **Contingency Planning** for Karthik’s absence includes handover to another team member to maintain continuity of support (49:40).

transcripts.title: Cerence x CultureMonkey - Employee Engagement Survey 2026 Discussion

summary.overview:
- **Survey readiness ahead of schedule:** Engagement survey setup is nearly complete, with a test phase starting soon.  

- **Participation strategy clarified:** Email participation is at 85%, so Microsoft Teams integration is deprioritized. QR codes will be used.  

- **Test participant management:** Yugi will create the test survey and provide instructions for managing participants via UI or CSV upload.  

- **Customer feedback collection:** Elizabeth to receive guidance on leaving reviews on Trustpilot and G2 to enhance Culture Monkey's reputation.  

- **Advance notice to employees:** An email notification will be sent two weeks before the survey launch to boost awareness.  

- **Ongoing communication support:** Yugi encourages Elizabeth to reach out with questions to ensure project flow and stakeholder satisfaction.
summary.action_items:
**Elizabeth Egan**
Review the email with video case study questions and schedule a 30-45 minute session next week to record it (10:20)
Attempt leaving reviews on Culture Monkey platforms using provided prompts and links; reach out if assistance is needed (10:55)
Upload or add test survey participants directly via provided UI or CSV after Yugi sends the test survey link and instructions (13:30)

**Yugi**
Create and send a test survey link to Elizabeth after the call with instructions on how to add participants (13:30)
Provide documentation and steps for Microsoft Teams integration to Elizabeth for IT team review (07:41)
Provide the QR code and generic link option for survey participation post-test survey (08:45)

**Karthik N**
Send prompts and links for Culture Monkey review platforms to Elizabeth via email (11:30)
Assist Elizabeth during the call or later on if she faces any issues leaving reviews (10:55)
Follow up next week for scheduling and executing the video case study session based on Elizabeth’s availability (10:20)
summary.notes:
## **Survey Preparation and Execution**

The team is on track to complete the engagement survey setup well ahead of schedule, ensuring readiness before communication starts.

- **Survey setup nearly complete with test phase next** (05:45)  
    - Elizabeth confirmed all questions are ready and only needs to finalize test participants.  
    - Yugi outlined that after the test survey, preview reports and feedback will be shared for final review.  
    - Email templates have been prepared based on last year’s version, confirmed by Elizabeth.  
    - Early completion allows Elizabeth to send an advance notice email to employees in two weeks.

- **Microsoft Teams integration proposed but deprioritized** (06:39)  
    - Yugi explained Teams integration would push reminders as private messages, boosting participation.  
    - Elizabeth noted this requires significant IT involvement and is not a current priority due to **85% email participation** success.  
    - As a simpler alternative, Yugi offered QR codes and generic links for survey access, which Elizabeth accepted.  
    - Final details on QR codes and communications will be shared after the test survey phase.

- **Test survey participant management clarified** (13:31)  
    - Yugi will create the test survey and send Elizabeth a link with instructions to upload or select participants.  
    - Options include manual UI input or bulk CSV upload for participant emails.  
    - This approach empowers Elizabeth to manage test participants independently.

## **Customer Feedback and Review Strategy**

The team aims to gather positive customer reviews to enhance Culture Monkey’s market reputation using simplified tools and support.

- **Review collection process introduced to Elizabeth** (10:13)  
    - Karthik offered to guide Elizabeth through leaving reviews on Trustpilot and G2 platforms during the call.  
    - He provided prompts based on previous client feedback emails to simplify drafting positive reviews.  
    - Voice note options are available on G2 to reduce time and effort.  
    - Elizabeth agreed to review and potentially leave feedback at her convenience.

- **Strategic rationale for reviews emphasized** (11:19)  
    - Karthik explained positive reviews help Culture Monkey appear favorably in AI-driven search results for employee engagement tools.  
    - The focus is on gathering authentic, minimal-effort, positive feedback from valued customers like Elizabeth.  
    - He committed to ongoing support to resolve any difficulties Elizabeth faces during the review process.

## **Communication and Coordination Efficiency**

Clear communication and tool support are prioritized to maximize efficiency and reduce delays in survey deployment.

- **Advance employee notification planned** (09:27)  
    - Elizabeth will send an email two weeks before the survey launch to alert employees, leveraging the early readiness.  
    - This communication strategy aims to boost awareness and participation rates.

- **Collaboration and follow-up procedures set** (14:37)  
    - Yugi encouraged Elizabeth to reach out via email for any outstanding questions beyond this call.  
    - This ensures ongoing support without blocking progress.  
    - The team’s responsiveness contributes to smooth project flow and stakeholder satisfaction.

- **Video case study scheduling deferred but planned** (09:51)  
    - Karthik reminded Elizabeth of a pending video case study discussion but acknowledged her current unavailability.  
    - He proposed scheduling a 30-45 minute session next week to complete it, showing flexibility and respect for client bandwidth.

- **Audit Process Success:** Month-long audit revealed only one minor error, improving data synchronization between Workday and Culture Monkey.

- **Bug Fixes Prioritized:** Two report builder bugs to be fixed by end of the week, ensuring smoother audits and data handling.

- **Launch of Candidate Surveys:** Candidate survey system live post-audit approval; legacy data will be downloaded after successful audit completion.

- **Training for Hiring Managers:** Training planned for dashboards within one to two weeks post-audit, ensuring smooth adoption of new survey system.

- **System Integrity Maintained:** Only candidate API integration paused; employee integrations remain active to prevent data loss during transition.

- **Clear Ownership Established:** Dana assigned to manage recruitment data reports, maintaining oversight to avoid skewed candidate score data.

summary.notes:
## **Audit Process and Data Integrity**

The team has established a robust audit process to ensure accurate data synchronization between Workday and Culture Monkey.

- **Month-long audit confirms data accuracy and process stability** (04:14)  
    - Scott Domes shared an automated audit system comparing Workday reports with Culture Monkey data.  
    - After nearly **four weeks of audits**, only one minor error was found and set aside, affirming data cleanliness.  
    - The plan is to reduce audit frequency to monthly or quarterly once consistent accuracy is confirmed.  
    - This system helps identify missing or mismatched data automatically, reducing manual workload.

- **Report builder and bug fixes to improve audit capabilities** (05:12)  
    - Karthik Rao reported two bugs affecting the report builder and confidentiality reporting, aiming to fix by **end of the week**.  
    - Access to the report builder will be limited to **super admins** for controlled data handling.  
    - Dana Matusek will coordinate with Kate to confirm availability of candidate recruitment data reports.  
    - These fixes are critical to enable ongoing audit and sanity checks without manual hurdles.

- **Sanity checks and test data removal planned before formal audits** (07:57)  
    - Dana suggested quick sanity checks every **3-5 days** to verify survey and automation functionality before full audits.  
    - Karthik confirmed removal of test participants is in progress and expected to complete by **tomorrow**.  
    - Scott agreed to let Dana’s team handle early sanity checks and report results, pending full report access.  
    - This phased approach ensures data quality before deeper audits begin.

## **Candidate and Hiring Manager Survey Transition**

The transition from Glint to Culture Monkey for candidate and hiring manager surveys is nearly complete with key approvals and system checks done.

- **Candidate survey system switched on post audit approval** (09:01)  
    - Patrick's audit last week and Kate’s approval on Monday cleared the candidate survey system for launch.  
    - Glint was turned off for candidate surveys, with data downloads planned after successful audit completion.  
    - Dana confirmed survey messages in Workday are shut off to avoid duplication.  
    - Scott will download legacy Glint data after confirming audit success to preserve historical data.

- **Hiring manager survey system setup and training planned next** (09:47)  
    - Training on dashboards for hiring managers is scheduled within **one to two weeks** after audit confirmation.  
    - This training is the next milestone following the candidate audit success.  
    - No shutdown was required on Glint for hiring manager surveys as it was not active there.  
    - Ensures smooth adoption and understanding of the new survey system within the team.

- **API integration adjustments to pause candidate data flow** (12:13)  
    - Patrick clarified that only the candidate API integration with Glint will be paused, not deleted, maintaining system integrity.  
    - Employee API integrations remain active as the exit survey in Glint is still running.  
    - This selective pause prevents data loss while allowing gradual system transition.  
    - The approach reduces risk of breaking existing processes during the switch.

## **Technical Support and Bug Resolution**

Resolving system bugs and providing access improvements are critical to maintaining smooth operations and audit accuracy.

- **Key bugs identified with fixes targeted early next week** (14:26)  
    - Karthik listed three bugs: Dana’s Culture Monkey access, report builder functionality, and candidate status visibility.  
    - The candidate active/inactive status bug impacts visibility into recruitment progress.  
    - All three issues are slated for resolution by **early next week** to avoid blocking audits.  
    - Scott assigned Karthik to document these bugs and track progress in shared files.

- **Engineering support planned for Culture Monkey access issue** (00:42)  
    - Karthik requested Dana’s help to coordinate a call with engineering to resolve her Culture Monkey access by Friday.  
    - This collaboration aims to fix a configuration issue impacting Dana’s ability to manage data.  
    - Timely resolution is important to maintain her role in audit and survey processes.  
    - This shows cross-team cooperation to resolve operational blockers quickly.

## **Process Coordination and Communication**

Clear communication and defined responsibilities are ensuring smooth progress through transition and audit phases.

- **Action item ownership clarified for ongoing tasks** (01:35)  
    - Kate’s recruiting and licensing issue was handed off to Dana to manage ongoing Evergreen process concerns.  
    - Scott emphasized maintaining umbrella PO oversight to avoid skewing candidate score data.  
    - Dana will liaise with Kate to gather necessary recruitment data reports.  
    - This delegation keeps key issues active without losing momentum.

- **Routine updates and status checks planned via Slack** (15:29)  
    - Scott requested updates on sanity check completion to be shared in the Slack channel.  
    - This keeps the broader team informed and aligned on audit progress.  
    - Enables quick response to any emerging issues or delays.  
    - Supports transparency and accountability across distributed team members.

- **Integration and data flow status explicitly tracked** (11:23)  
    - Patrick confirmed both candidate and employee integrations with Glint are still running daily.  
    - Scott added a note to pause candidate data flow without deleting APIs.  
    - This tracking prevents accidental data loss and supports controlled migration.  
    - Shows careful coordination to maintain data integrity during system changes.

    - The current working list holds about **40 questions** after removing redundancies, awaiting Janet’s finalized version with all updates.
- **Driver mapping alignment to Inga’s engagement model categories** was reviewed and confirmed (15:12)
    - Janet categorized all questions under five key drivers: **Me and My Work, Trusted Leaders, My Manager, Positive Environment, and Learning and Growing**.
    - Karthik explained these categories correspond to Culture Monkey drivers on their platform, ensuring benchmarking consistency.
    - Once Janet sends her final sheet including driver mapping, Karthik will update the backend and rename drivers accordingly.
- **Technical issue with spreadsheet versions** delayed finalization but will be resolved by Janet locating the correct file (07:05)
    - Janet’s Mac system required exporting which complicated syncing changes.
    - Karthik suggested proceeding with the current version for now and updating once Janet shares the correct sheet.
- **Plan to upload finalized questions to the platform and preview survey** was agreed (17:54)
    - After acknowledgment of the final question list and driver mapping, Karthik will upload questions.
    - Janet will review a preview link to confirm question layout before moving to the next phase.

## **Employee Data and Survey Channels**

The next phase involves preparing employee data and deciding on communication channels to maximize survey participation.

- **Employee data submission will follow question finalization** to ensure data validation and quality (17:54)
    - Karthik requested the full organization employee dataset after the survey questions are set.
    - He will validate the data for duplicates and completeness to improve report insights.
    - The validation step may take an extra day before full processing.
- **Survey distribution channels discussed to ensure maximum reach** across the organization (20:02)
    - Janet confirmed **email and WhatsApp** as primary channels, since not all employees have email access.
    - Slack was ruled out due to inconsistent usage; Microsoft Teams is a secondary option.
    - Karthik advised verifying that mobile numbers correspond to employees’ primary WhatsApp numbers to avoid delivery issues.
- **Technical setup for Teams distribution requires IT involvement** (21:24)
    - Karthik offered to provide setup instructions for Janet’s IT team once employee data is ready.
    - This multi-channel approach aims to **increase survey participation and improve insights quality**.
- **Whitelisting domain with IT teams is in progress to prevent delivery blocks** (18:46)
    - Karthik mentioned an email sent to Janet about whitelisting domains to ensure survey emails reach employees.
    - Testing and a pilot survey batch of 5-10 people are planned before full launch to catch any delivery or system issues early.

## **Survey Launch Timeline and Cadence**

The team set a tentative timeline and regular check-ins to keep the survey project on track.

- **Target launch timeframe set for mid to late May** with flexibility for earlier pilot or phased rollout (25:37)
    - Janet prefers launching for one or two business units initially before full rollout.
    - Karthik confirmed that a full launch in April is possible but depends on final readiness.
    - The survey implementation itself is expected to take about **10 to 14 days** once questions and data are ready.
- **Weekly cadence calls agreed to track progress and resolve blockers** (22:44)
    - Next call scheduled for **April 1st at 9:30 AM UK time** to review question finalization and data status.
    - Karthik emphasized these check-ins will help maintain momentum and clear pending items.
- **Pilot survey planned after question upload and data validation** to test all technical and process aspects (18:46)
    - Small pilot will check email delivery, access, and survey experience.
    - Feedback from pilot will guide any final fixes before wider launch.
- **Clear division of responsibilities and phased approach to reduce risk** (17:54)
    - Janet focuses on finalizing questions and providing employee data.
    - Karthik manages platform updates, data validation, whitelisting coordination, and survey deployment.
    - This breakdown ensures smooth flow and quick resolution of issues.

## **Platform and Process Insights**

The discussion included how the platform supports driver mapping and survey benchmarks to deliver actionable insights.

- **Culture Monkey driver mapping integrated with Inga’s engagement categories** to align survey results with known benchmarks (15:12)
    - Karthik showed how platform driver names will be renamed to Inga’s categories for easier interpretation.
    - This linkage allows comparing survey results against broader benchmarks for context.
- **Preview functionality on platform allows review before final deployment** (17:54)
    - Janet will receive a preview link to verify question display and flow.
    - This step minimizes errors and surprises during live survey.
- **Quality assurance includes validation of employee data and pilot survey** to ensure data accuracy and smooth operation (18:46)
    - Duplicate checks and completeness reviews will improve report quality.
    - Pilot survey results will confirm email and channel effectiveness.
- **Whitelisting and multi-channel delivery reflect a focus on technical robustness and participation** (18:46, 20:02)
    - These setup steps reduce risk of delivery failures and boost response rates.
    - Multiple channels cater to diverse employee access scenarios, especially where email is limited.

transcripts.title: Employee Engagement Platform : update  the UAT progress

summary.overview:
- **Access Rules for Sub Admins:** Sub admin access requires matching team names, locations, and business units; can't view outside permissions.  
- **Thailand Locations:** There are 24 locations in Thailand; comprehensive team mapping is essential for each sub admin's access.  
- **Lifecycle Survey Feedback:** Reducing survey questions is crucial to improve response rates; a test launch will finalize the question set.  
- **Integration of New Countries:** Mongolia and Australia will join later; adding employees is a simple update, no full UAT needed.  
- **UAT Timeline Urgency:** UAT completion is essential before April 1 committee meeting to avoid delays; immediate testing is recommended.  
- **Strategic Presentation:** The team passed the initial corporate review; a 45-minute presentation will explain the new platform approach to the main committee.
summary.action_items:
**Mouriya S**
Provide sub admin access with all team names and business units enabled for user Natan to test UAT functionality (37:11)
Share minimized lifecycle survey question set and related UAT test scenarios within 1–2 days (34:21)
Resend invite email to sub admins who have not logged in to ensure access confirmation (22:55)
Provide translation templates for lifecycle survey questions by end of the day (28:02)
Assist by enabling sub admin and manager access for identified employees in Thailand and China (37:35)

**Rintarn Jaroenroop**
Coordinate with Natan to verify sub admin access and confirm UAT results for lifecycle and access permissions (36:44)
Communicate internally to update team name and business unit details, ensuring alignment for access permissions (10:46)
Check with HR/data teams regarding extra countries (Mongolia, Centennial team) to prepare data files for future platform inclusion (30:44)
Present in upcoming main corporate governance committee meeting using provided methodology slides (40:50)
Complete UAT for sub admins and managers this week per schedule to support committee negotiation (38:14)
summary.notes:
## **Access Permissions and Sub Admin Setup**

The team clarified the access permission rules for sub admins, emphasizing the need to match team names, locations, and business units for visibility (02:26).  
- **Mouriya S** explained that sub admin access depends on a combined match of **team name, location, and business unit**, which controls what data and reports the sub admins can see.  
- This combination cannot be overridden, meaning sub admins cannot view reports outside their allowed team and location matches.  
- To allow sub admins to see multiple teams or locations, the relevant **team names and business units must be explicitly added** to their access list.  
- **Rintarn Jaroenroop** noted this differs from their five-year practice where HR could oversee multiple teams, revealing a need to adjust the access setup to match platform rules.  

A practical approach was agreed to add all team names related to a location rather than overriding platform rules, simplifying access management (09:25).  
- Mouriya suggested adding multiple team names to a sub admin’s profile to enable broader report visibility within the platform’s framework.  
- This adjustment means **sub admins’ access will be more comprehensive but must be configured carefully** with accurate team and business unit data.  
- Rintarn acknowledged the extra workload but preferred this method over overriding rules.  
- They discussed the total number of locations in Thailand as **24**, which requires comprehensive mapping of teams for each sub admin.  

China’s sub admin access configuration was noted as more complex due to business unit divisions like power plants and solar farms (15:11).  
- Mouriya and Rintarn reviewed the China sub admin entries and identified the need for precise location and business unit matching.  
- Anonymity thresholds requiring a minimum of **5 employees per combined category** limit visibility for some managers with fewer reports.  
- The platform does not show managers their own scores but only their reportees’, aligning with privacy rules.  

Testing and rollout plans for sub admin access were set with a pilot sub admin enabled for verification (14:10).  
- Mouriya will send the login details for the pilot sub admin to Rintarn for testing access and data accuracy.  
- Rintarn will coordinate with **Nathan** to confirm the access works and then approve enabling the same setup for all other sub admins.  
- This approach aims to complete the access permissions UAT within **1-2 days** and proceed to next steps promptly.  

## **Lifecycle Survey and Question Optimization**

The team discussed concerns about the high number of questions in the lifecycle survey and its impact on employee response rates (24:54).  
- Rintarn shared feedback from multiple countries requesting the **reduction of questions per milestone** to avoid survey fatigue.  
- Mouriya agreed that fewer questions would improve engagement and yield more actionable insights.  
- She explained that once live, the lifecycle survey questions cannot be changed, so a test launch is essential.  

A phased approach was agreed to handle question minimization and payment timing (26:03).  
- Mouriya proposed doing a **test launch first** to finalize the question set before going live with payment.  
- Rintarn emphasized the digital committee’s strong requirement that all features be tested by UAT before going live and payment release.  
- They agreed to proceed with lifecycle UAT while awaiting payment, planning to finalize questions after testing.  

Mouriya committed to reducing repeated questions and sharing a minimized question preview within **1-2 days** (34:21).  
- She has already removed duplicates from combined stages and will further trim questions as requested.  
- Rintarn appreciated this support, noting the importance of all questions but needing expert help to reduce them.  

Additional survey wording changes were requested for localization (28:58).  
- Rintarn asked to replace “call” with “contact” in the first 30 days survey question to reflect practices in countries like Japan.  
- Mouriya acknowledged this and will update the wording accordingly.  

## **Integration and Future Employee Additions**

The team reviewed the current integration scope and future plans for including new countries like Mongolia and Australia (30:02).  
- Rintarn noted Mongolian and Centennial (Australia) employees are currently not in the platform due to integration exclusions.  
- Mouriya confirmed that when these countries join, their employee data can be added by updating the integration feed from HRMS.  
- Adding new employees later does not require a full UAT; it is a simple incremental update that can be done anytime before future surveys.  

The timeline for adding new countries was estimated conservatively (33:48).  
- Rintarn mentioned a potential survey inclusion by **September**, allowing months to prepare data.  
- Mouriya advised submitting data at least a month before the survey launch, ensuring smooth integration.  

## **UAT Testing and Timeline Coordination**

The UAT process for sub admin access and manager roles was clarified with next steps and responsibilities (21:29).  
- Mouriya has provided one manager access and one sub admin access to start UAT immediately.  
- Rintarn will coordinate with Nathan and others to confirm that sub admins can log in and see accurate data.  
- Once confirmed, the same access settings will be extended to all sub admins, including those in China.  

The urgency around UAT completion was tied to payment and committee scheduling (38:14).  
- Rintarn explained the **committee presentation scheduled for April 1** depends on completing UAT this week.  
- The committee had delayed their queue due to late sign-off documents for multiple projects, increasing pressure to finish UAT quickly.  
- Mouriya emphasized that timely confirmation of sub admin access would help meet deadlines and recommended using current access credentials for testing immediately.  

Documentation and process clarifications were provided to ease UAT (39:41).  
- Rintarn confirmed no additional UAT documents are needed for sub admin access updates, simplifying the workflow.  
- Mouriya reassured that adding new employees is straightforward and does not require repeated UAT cycles.  

## **Strategic Presentation and Change Management**

Rintarn shared that the team successfully passed the corporate governance committee’s initial review for the platform change (40:50).  
- The next step is a **45-minute presentation to the main committee**, where the new platform and methodology will be explained.  
- The committee is expected to question the methodology, especially since the approach changed from consultant-led to platform-based.  

Mouriya advised that the existing documentation on algorithms and methodology is sufficient for the presentation (40:50).  
- She recommended Rintarn lead the presentation to maintain control and offered follow-up support for any questions after the meeting.  
- This approach reflects confidence in the platform’s transparency and the team’s readiness to address concerns.  

This presentation marks a major change in vendor strategy and is a crucial milestone for the project’s success (41:31).  
- The transition to a platform provider model is a key strategic shift for the organization’s employee engagement and survey approach.  
- Success here will influence future adoption and the integration of new survey features.

summary.overview:
- **Contract Termination:** Vendor contracts, including Culture Monkey, to end by April 1st due to company downsizing and merger.

- **Employee Count:** Workforce reduced from 200 to 38, with most departures by March month-end amid merger.

- **Financial Challenges:** Culture Monkey's contract, valued at 1.8 lakh INR, has complexities for termination due to invoicing terms.

- **Negotiation for Settlement:** Both parties to honor payment for April, seeking a credit note for remainder to maintain goodwill.

- **Broader Vendor Closure:** Multiple vendor relationships ending without disputes, making Culture Monkey's payment issues unique.

- **Next Steps:** Continued discussions with finance teams aim to finalize a fair settlement before the contract ends March 31st.
summary.action_items:
**Gowtham Kamaraj**
Discuss contract termination financial implications with finance head and leadership (17:40)
Explore possible invoice reduction or credit note proposal and communicate updates via email (17:40)
Loop finance head into email conversations with Reverie for follow-up (17:40)

**Mohit Kumar**
Await finance team response and proposal from Culture Monkey for mutual contract closure (17:53)
summary.notes:
## **Contract Termination Amid Merger**

The company is actively downsizing and merging with its parent firm, prompting a request to end vendor contracts, including the one with Culture Monkey, by April 1st. (01:02)
- **Mohit Kumar** explained the workforce shrinkage from **200 to 38 employees**, with most leaving by the end of March due to the merger.
- The merger process is expected to complete in **two to three months**, influencing the decision to close external vendor services and customer contracts.
- The company halted Culture Monkey surveys since last June as the integration and communication from JPL delayed decisions until February.
- The decision to cease Culture Monkey’s services stems from no employee base needing the service post-merger and JPL having its own systems, making continuation redundant.

## **Contractual and Financial Challenges**

Culture Monkey raised concerns over the difficulty of reversing a recently invoiced two-year contract, complicating the termination request. (04:26)
- **Gowtham Kamaraj** highlighted the contract as an annual renewable with a **two-year term** and a **60-day notice** clause, emphasizing the invoice was already raised.
- The invoice value discussed was approximately **1.8 lakh INR** for the yearly subscription, payable from April 1st.
- Mohit expressed challenges in paying for unused services, seeking a fair resolution that aligns with audit and financial compliance.
- Gowtham acknowledged the issue but noted finance teams and investors are involved, making contract reversal complex.

## **Negotiation for Mutual Settlement**

Both parties agreed to seek a middle ground by honoring a notice period payment while closing the contract amicably to maintain future goodwill. (08:09)
- Mohit proposed paying for the month of April only and receiving a credit note for the remainder, framing it like an employment notice period.
- Gowtham committed to discussing with the finance head to explore possible reductions or credit notes but noted the difficulty due to prior invoicing.
- Mohit emphasized the importance of an amicable end to preserve future business relationships and referrals despite the current shutdown.
- Both acknowledged the contractual nature of SaaS subscriptions but expressed willingness to find a fair compromise given the unusual merger circumstances.

## **Context of Broader Vendor Closures**

The termination request is part of a larger vendor shutdown strategy related to the merger, with other partners not opposing contract closures. (13:47)
- Mohit shared that multiple vendor relationships, including HRMS and ATS platforms, are being ended without disputes.
- This distinct pushback from Culture Monkey on payment obligations is unique compared to other vendors.
- Gowtham confirmed the standard SaaS subscription model involves upfront payment unrelated to actual usage.
- Mohit reiterated the company’s financial constraints and audit requirements necessitate closure and fair billing reconciliation.

## **Next Steps for Resolution**

Both sides agreed to continue discussions with finance teams to finalize a fair settlement and avoid legal escalation or payment disputes. (17:40)
- Gowtham promised to loop in the finance head and update via email about possible invoice reductions or credit note issuance.
- Mohit firmly stated that small discounts like 10-15% are insufficient and that a full credit note with payment for one or two months is the preferred solution.
- Both parties expressed intent to avoid prolonged conflicts, emphasizing professionalism and mutual respect.
- The resolution aims to conclude the contract by March 31st, with agreed payments settled promptly to close the matter cleanly.
